labormarket 0.1.0
  • Get Started
    • Introduction
    • Analyzing a Simulated Labor Market
  • Reference

labormarket

labormarket is an R package for simulating worker-firm matched datasets and correcting limited mobility bias in two-way fixed effects models. It is designed as a teaching and research tool for labor economics.

Installation

Install the development version from GitHub:

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("hugosantanna/labormarket")

Quick Start

Simulate a Labor Market 

library(labormarket)

# Simulate a labor market with 6 firm types, 10 worker types, 4 periods, 100k workers
lm_obj <- simlabormarket(nk = 6, nl = 10, nt = 4, ni = 100000, lambda = 0.05)

# Access the panel data
panel <- lm_obj@panel
head(panel)

Correct Limited Mobility Bias

After fitting a two-way fixed effects (AKM) model, use lmbias() to correct the limited mobility bias following Azkarate-Askasua and Zerecero (2022):

library(fixest)

# Fit an AKM model
panel$fid <- as.factor(panel$fid)
panel$i   <- as.factor(panel$i)
model <- feols(lw ~ 1 | i + fid, data = panel)

# Add predicted values
panel$xbeta <- predict(model, type = "link")

# Bootstrap bias correction
results <- lmbias(model, panel, R = 100)
print(results)

References

  • Azkarate-Askasua, M. and Zerecero, M. (2022). “The Macroeconomics of Firms’ Financing.”
